question: What health services are available at the campus Health Centre
answer: The campus Health Centre provides general medical consultations routine checkups chronic condition support and minor injury treatment Students can also access advice on medications vaccinations and referrals to external specialists if needed

question: Do students need to register with the Health Centre before using its services
answer: Yes students should register with the Health Centre as soon as they arrive on campus Registration allows faster appointment booking and ensures staff have the necessary medical information

question: How do I book an appointment with a doctor or nurse at the Health Centre
answer: Appointments can be booked online through the student portal by phone or in person at reception Urgent cases may be offered same day appointments depending on availability

question: What should I do if I need medical help outside Health Centre hours
answer: If the Health Centre is closed you can use the local urgent care service or a national out of hours helpline For emergencies you should contact emergency services directly

question: Are mental health counselling services provided on campus
answer: Yes the university offers confidential mental health counselling delivered by qualified therapists Students can receive support for stress anxiety low mood academic pressure and personal challenges

question: How long is the waiting time for mental health appointments
answer: Waiting time varies but the university aims to offer an initial consultation within two weeks Urgent cases may receive faster support or be directed to external crisis services

question: What wellbeing workshops or group sessions are available to students
answer: Workshops cover topics such as stress management sleep routines mindfulness resilience and healthy living Group sessions are free and designed to support student wellbeing

question: Can I access support for disabilities or long term health conditions
answer: Yes the Disability Support Service offers personalised guidance learning adjustments and accommodation recommendations Students can receive help with documentation and ongoing wellbeing plans

question: What should I do if I need academic adjustments for health reasons
answer: You should contact the Disability Support Service with relevant medical evidence An advisor will help create a personalised support plan such as extra time flexible deadlines or alternative formats

question: Are there nutrition or diet support services for students
answer: Yes students can book appointments with a nutrition advisor who helps with meal planning healthy habits and dietary needs Workshops are offered throughout the year

question: Does the university gym provide fitness assessments or personal training
answer: Yes the gym offers optional fitness assessments covering strength cardiovascular fitness and flexibility Students can request personalised exercise plans to support their goals

question: Is there a cost for attending group fitness classes at the gym
answer: Most group classes are included with student membership while some specialist sessions may have a small additional fee Booking is done online through the class system

question: What support is available for students recovering from injury
answer: The sports therapy team provides rehabilitation advice guided exercises and injury assessments They work with the Health Centre to create safe recovery programmes

question: Do you offer support for exam stress and academic pressure
answer: Yes the wellbeing team provides one to one sessions focused on managing stress building study routines and balancing work demands Workshops are offered during the exam season

question: What should I do if I am worried about a friends mental health
answer: You should contact the wellbeing team who can provide guidance on how to support your friend Staff can help assess urgency and direct you to suitable resources

question: Can international students use the same health and wellbeing services as others
answer: Yes all students have equal access to health counselling and wellbeing support The team also offers help with cultural adjustment and settling into university life

question: What should I do if I feel unsafe on campus
answer: You should contact campus security who operate twenty four seven They can provide immediate assistance safety escorts and support with incident reporting

question: Are there workshops focused on improving sleep and healthy routines
answer: Yes sleep wellbeing workshops teach practical techniques for better sleep Topics include night routines screen use and understanding sleep cycles

question: How can I access support for substance misuse concerns
answer: Students can access confidential help through specialist advisors who provide guidance on risk reduction treatment options and behaviour change They can also refer you to external services

question: What resources support general emotional wellbeing
answer: The university offers self help guides wellbeing toolkits relaxation audio and online modules on stress motivation and emotional regulation All materials are free through the wellbeing portal

question: How do I request a repeat prescription through the Health Centre
answer: You can request a repeat prescription by submitting a form through the student portal or by visiting reception Staff will process the request and contact you when it is ready for collection

question: Does the university offer physiotherapy services
answer: Yes the university provides physiotherapy appointments for assessment treatment and guided rehabilitation Students can book sessions for sports injuries posture issues and chronic pain

question: What should I do if I feel overwhelmed by academic workload
answer: You can book a study support session with the wellbeing team who can help you create a realistic study plan They also offer techniques for managing pressure and improving focus

question: Are there peer support groups available for students
answer: Yes the wellbeing centre runs peer groups where students meet to discuss challenges share advice and build community These sessions are facilitated by trained student volunteers

question: How can I learn more about maintaining a healthy work life balance
answer: Work life balance workshops run each term teaching time management boundaries and self care strategies Students can also access online guides with practical tips

question: What services are offered for students experiencing homesickness
answer: The wellbeing team provides one to one emotional support and practical guidance for adapting to university life Social events and peer groups also help students feel more connected

question: How do I access sexual health services on campus
answer: Sexual health clinics visit campus regularly offering free testing contraception and advice Appointments are confidential and can be booked online

question: Does the Health Centre provide vaccinations
answer: Yes the Health Centre offers routine vaccinations including flu and travel related vaccines Availability may vary so students should book early during peak times

question: What should I do if I think I need an urgent mental health appointment
answer: If you feel at risk or highly distressed you should contact the wellbeing team immediately who will prioritise your case In severe situations emergency services may be required

question: Are there resources to help improve my sleep schedule
answer: Yes the wellbeing portal includes sleep guides audio relaxation tracks and step by step plans You can also attend sleep workshops for personalised advice

question: What fitness facilities are available beyond the main gym
answer: Students can access sports halls climbing walls courts and fitness studios Additional outdoor facilities include running tracks and training fields

question: Is there support for managing chronic fatigue or long term exhaustion
answer: Yes specialist advisors help create personalised routines that balance activity pacing rest and academic responsibilities They also coordinate with medical services for ongoing monitoring

question: How can I build healthier eating habits during busy study periods
answer: The nutrition advisor can help you plan quick balanced meals and snacks Practical workshops also teach budget friendly cooking methods

question: Does the wellbeing team help with social anxiety
answer: Yes the team offers strategies for confidence building exposure planning and communication skills Group sessions may also support social interaction in a safe environment

question: Are there programmes that teach mindfulness or meditation
answer: Yes weekly mindfulness sessions are open to all students and guided by trained practitioners Online recordings are also available for self guided practice

question: What should I do if I am struggling to adapt to university life
answer: You can arrange a settling in appointment with a wellbeing advisor who can offer emotional support and practical suggestions Social groups and community events also help students integrate

question: Can I receive support for financial stress as part of wellbeing services
answer: Yes wellbeing advisors can help you understand financial pressures and signpost you to hardship funding budgeting tools and student advice teams

question: How can I stay active if I am new to exercise
answer: The gym offers beginner friendly classes and introductory fitness programmes Coaches can help you build confidence and learn safe movement patterns

question: What should I do if illness causes me to miss assessments
answer: You should contact your academic school and the wellbeing team who can guide you through extension requests or mitigation processes Medical evidence may be required

question: Are there services that help students build resilience
answer: Yes resilience workshops teach coping skills emotional regulation and problem solving techniques Students can also access personalised coaching through wellbeing advisors
